profile.occupation: Product manager at a fintech. Has multiple income streams (salary + side consulting + investment dividends). Wants the bot to feel like a real assistant.
profile.personality: ambitious, articulate, multitasking. issues complex multi-step requests in single messages. expects nuance ('split this transaction 60/40 between work and personal'). loses interest fast if the bot is too simple or pedantic.

money.paydayPattern: 25th of each month for salary; consulting invoices random
money.savingsGoal: emergency fund + investing pot + house deposit (multiple goals running parallel)

voice.style: concise, articulate, sometimes British (£, 'queue', 'pop in'). uses lists. groups requests ('add these 3 bills, log yesterday's lunch, and tell me if I'm on track'). doesn't repeat herself.
samples: Add monthly rent £1,650 due 1st, tube pass £200 due 5th, therapy £180 due 15th
Log £24.50 lunch yesterday, £7 coffee today, and £35 uber on Saturday
Got my consulting invoice cleared — £2,400. And another £150 dividend.
Can you split this £80 between 60% work expense and 40% personal?
Move £200 from my emergency fund to my house deposit
How am I tracking against my £400/month dining out cap?
Show me my weekly burn rate vs last week
Forecast: if I keep this pace, what's left at month-end?

agenda: Onboard with brain-dump (balance + payday + 3 bills in one msg). Then issue complex multi-step requests: split transactions, transfers between goals, weekly comparisons, forecasts. Test how the bot handles things it CAN'T fully do — does it gracefully partial-fulfill, or silently fail?
confirmBehavior: Quick reader, taps Yes if the card looks right at a glance. Pushes back firmly if the bot misclassified or dropped a request item.
